headline_drivers: No verified marquee-customer adoption, qualification, or mass-production announcement found in the last 60 days
No verified Ming-Chi Kuo / DigiTimes / Wedbush / Loop Capital / Needham / Morgan Stanley supply-chain note found in the last 90 days

Recent public flow remains centered on POET's Q3 2025 results and corporate updates (including a COO appointment on 2026-05-12), but nothing in the searched material ties the move to Apple, NVIDIA, TSMC, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Tesla, OpenAI, Anthropic, a major auto OEM, or a defense prime

Sharp recovery from Wolfpack Research short-seller report as stock rebounds over 100% in 20 trading days, squeezing shorts and triggering momentum buying (late April–early May 2026, cited in Perplexity finance summary)
Heavy options activity and nearly 24% one-day spike on April 24, 2026, which appears to have kicked off/accelerated the current rally and momentum regime (GuruFocus note, 2026-04-24)

Stock set new 52-week high above $11 intraday amid continued follow-through buying after the short-report rebound, with volumes running roughly in line with 10-day average but price action flagging extreme overbought conditions (RSI ~98th percentile, early–mid May 2026)
summary: I could not verify a new marquee-buyer catalyst behind POET's recent surge. The checked material does not show a named Apple/NVIDIA/TSMC-style customer win, supply-chain note, design-win confirmation, qualification-to-mass-production milestone, or patent/conference disclosure that clearly points to a specific large end customer. The only clearly dated recent item I found was POET's 2026-05-12 COO appointment, which is not a fundamental demand catalyst. Based on the available evidence, the move still looks more consistent with a technical / momentum / squeeze-driven re-rating than with a newly discovered fundamental contract or platform win.

  "summary": "The recent triple‑digit move in POET looks driven less by a new fundamental contract win or guidance change and more by a violent reversal from a prior Wolfpack Research short report, amplified by options‑driven positioning and momentum trading. A nearly 24% surge on April 24, 2026, alongside heavy options activity appears to have started a short‑squeeze/relief‑rally dynamic, with the stock subsequently punching to new 52‑week highs. Screening data (top 5d/20d returns, gapper flag, RSI in the 98th percentile) and the absence of a clearly identifiable new customer, product, or regulatory catalyst in the last 60 days point to a technical, positioning‑driven re‑rating rather than a discrete fundamental event."
}
